title: Pashvi (species)
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[Species/Legends|species]]|the [[Pashvi system/Legends|Pashvi system]] or the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Pashvi/Legends|Pashvi]]}} {{Species |image= |name=Pashvi |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]][[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']], Chapter 8 |class= |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor= |haircolor= |feathers= |eyecolor= |distinctions=*[[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoid]], with a wide torso and/or long [[Limb/Legends|arms]] *Distant emotional state |lifespan= |origin=[[Pashvi/Legends|Pashvi]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}{{JCFCite|url=posts/27848315|board=Literature|thread=The Essential Atlas and Galactic Cartography: Official Discussion|user=[[Jason Fry|jasonfry]]|date=2007-12-11|time=7:59 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20170916153037/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-essential-atlas-and-galactic-cartography-official-discussion.21938930/page-43|quote=I wouldn't get your hopes up re alien homeworlds, beyond simple stuff like the Planetnamian species getting a Planetnamia on the map or things Dan and I can account for with a relatively quick reference.|archivefile=File:JasonFry-JCFAtlasHomeworlds.png}} [[Jason Fry]], co-author of ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', stated his intention to create homeworlds for numerous species based on context implied from their names. Pashvi appears to be one such case. |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|They've got a system on the edge of Wild Space. I've been there a few times—there's a small but stable market for their art, mostly in the Corporate Sector.|Dubrak Qennto|}} The '''Pashvi''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] whose bodies, while largely [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]], featured a wider torso and/or longer [[Limb/Legends|arms]] than a [[Human/Legends|Human]]. They lived on a [[Pashvi/Legends|planet]] in the [[Pashvi system/Legends|Pashvi system]] of [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]], the surface of which was dotted with many thousands of rock pillars, atop which grew both edible [[Plant/Legends|plants]] and extremely dangerous [[Unidentified Pashvi avian|flying predators]]. That led to the Pashvi becoming both attracted to and frightened of the pillars and, more generally, made them keep their emotional distance from people and things they valued. Pashvi were generally peaceable beings with little skill or inclination for fighting. Their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was [[Vagaari raids|raided]] by the [[Vagaari/Legends|Vagaari]] [[Vagaari Empire|nomads]] some point prior to [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]]. Pashvi art had a stable but small market, mostly in the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]]. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|The artist is humanoid, proportioned differently from humans and Chiss, with either a wider torso or longer arms. There's something of a distance to his emotional state, too. I would say his people are both drawn to and yet repulsed by or fearful of the physical objects they live among.|Thrawn, upon encountering Pashvi sculpture|}} Pashvi were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]], although a typical member of the [[Species/Legends|species]] had longer [[Limb/Legends|arms]] and/or a wider torso than those of a [[Human/Legends|Human]] or [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]]. Crops made up at least a portion of the Pashvi diet.{{CSWECite|III|14|}} ==Society and culture== {{Quote|Their world is sprinkled with thousands of rock pillars. Most of the best food plants grow on the tops. Unfortunately, so does a nasty predator avian. It makes for—well, for pretty much just what he said.|Dubrak Qennto|}} Pashvi were known to be an amicable and peaceable species with little inclination or skill at fighting or violence. Their [[Pashvi/Legends|homeworld's]] terrain was marked by thousands of scattered stone pillars. The tops of these received more sunlight than the bottoms, which made the food [[Plant/Legends|plants]] growing there more nutritious and thus highly desirable to the Pashvi, forming the staple of their diet. However, the tops of those natural formations were also home to a [[Unidentified Pashvi avian|predatory species of avians]]. Accordingly, the Pashvi attitude toward the rock pillars was one of both reverence and fear. More broadly, their experience with the pillars led Pashvi to keep their distance emotionally from one another and from things of importance to them. The Pashvi created sculptures. That art form came in diverse styles, including one that used [[Color/Legends|blue]] and white materials. By [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]], Pashvi [[Artist/Legends|artists]] had begun exporting some of that work offworld, and a small collectors' market opened up, particularly in the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]]. Despite the remoteness of their world, the species received occasional visits from outsiders, although their world was beyond the reach of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] or the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] in 27 BBY. ==History== {{Quote|I presume these Pashvi won't have put up much resistance to Vagaari raids?''"
"''Hardly. They're a pretty agreeable people. Lousy at fighting.|Thrawn and Dubrak Qennto|}} [[File:Outboundthrawn.jpg|thumb|247px|right|upright|Mitth'raw'nuruodo discovered a cache of Pashvi art works on a Vagaari vessel.]] The Pashvi hailed from a planet in the [[Pashvi system/Legends|Pashvi system]], located on the frontier of [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]]. Due to the fact that the rock pillars of the world provided both sustenance and potential [[Death/Legends|death]], the Pashvi developed a distanced emotional state that had them keep friends and valuables at a safe emotional distance. At some point by 27 BBY, the Pashvi [[Vagaari raids|fell prey]] to [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] of the marauding [[Vagaari/Legends|Vagaari]] species. The invaders plundered thirteen sculptures from the Pashvi and placed them in the hold of their [[Vagaari plunder ship|starship]]. Later, that vessel came under the control of the Chiss [[Force Commander]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Mitth'raw'nuruodo]]—also [[Core name/Legends|known]] as Thrawn—of the [[Chiss Expansionary Fleet/Legends|Chiss Expansionary Fleet]]. The [[Chiss officer/Legends|officer]] found the [[Unidentified Pashvi sculpture|Pashvi sculptures]] in the [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] treasure hold, and by analyzing the works' forms, surmised precise details about the Pashvi creators. The show impressed the Human [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Dubrak Qennto]], with whom Thrawn was traveling. ==Behind the scenes== Author [[Timothy Zahn]] created the Pashvi for the novel [[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']], published by [[Del Rey]] in [[2006]]. Although no member of the species appears in the novel, Zahn's protagonist, Thrawn, discovers a cache of Pashvi artwork. Thrawn surmises from the Pashvi's art that they must be humanoid beings with either longer arms or a wider torso than that of a Human or Chiss, a notion with which Quennto agrees. However, the book does not specify which, if any, of Thrawn's conclusions is correct. The species is referenced in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]]. ==Appearances== *[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] {{Ab|Outbound Flight (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1stm|in book}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Pashvi }} [[Category:Wild Space sentient species]]
